title: Garp’s Last Stand on Hachinosu
description: On pages 10–12, this part focuses on Unknown person #10, Marines, Unknown person #11, Bepo, Unknown person #12, Prince Grus as Garp and SWORD fight the Blackbeard Pirates on Hachinosu while Koby’s rescue becomes a turning point for the next generation of Marines.
scene: Garp and SWORD fight the Blackbeard Pirates on Hachinosu while Koby’s rescue becomes a turning point for the next generation of Marines.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: UHmm... Actually, Koby asked me to be quiet about this...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm just a normal person, I don't have any talent for fighting...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Increasing the effort tenfold is not enough! I have to do it 100, no, 200 times more than anyone else!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I've got to train harder! So I can become an outstanding marine

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, would you look at that! After training with me, he's doing even more?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Yes sir, he does it every night!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: continuously with his blood- soaked hands!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Kobby- Senpa!! It's dangerous OVA! There!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and you will see the terrible sight of marines screaming towards their deaths!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And even if they escape to the sea, Aokui will freeze them!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Let's escape! We can't do anything about this!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If I can't destroy this, everyone will die!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: because of me, everyone will die!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But what are we gonna do about the debris?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Since when did you have this kind of power?!

summary: This part advances the Garp’s Last Stand at Hachinosu through 44 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
